---
title: "Setup"
---

Before starting the lessons, make sure the computer has the tools needed to run Python.

## What you need

You need:

- **Python**: the programming language.
- **Positron**: the coding environment where you write and run Python programs.
- **Quarto**: only needed if you want to render this course website yourself.

## Folder structure

Create a folder for your course work, for example:

```text
PythonCourseWork/
├── lesson1.py
├── lesson2.py
├── games/
└── experiments/
```

Put your Python files in this folder.

## Check that Python works

Open a terminal in Positron and type:

```bash
python --version
```

You should see a Python version number.

Then create a file called:

```text
hello.py
```

with this code:

```python
print("Hello, Python!")
```

Run it from the terminal:

```bash
python hello.py
```

If you see:

```text
Hello, Python!
```

then Python is working.

::: {.callout-note}
## On some Windows computers

You may need to type `py hello.py` instead of `python hello.py`.
:::

## How to use this course locally

To build the full website, open this folder in Positron and run:

```bash
quarto render
```

The finished website will appear in:

```text
_book/
```

Open:

```text
_book/index.html
```

in your browser.
